title: Investigate childBasePos padding double-count in TreeCursor.GotoFirstChild
description:
Pre-existing issue found during wcu.1 fix review (eaf840a).

GotoFirstChild (tree_cursor.go:71) computes:
  childBasePos = LengthAdd(entry.position, padding)

But the C tree-sitter cursor starts child iteration at entry.position (before padding), not entry.position + padding. Since SummarizeChildren sets parent.padding = first_child.padding, adding padding before iterating children means the first child's position is double-counted.

This doesn't trigger in current tests because all first children have 0 padding. But for trees where the first visible child has non-zero padding, Node.StartByte() and cursor positions would be wrong.

Also affects: findFirstVisibleChild's hidden node recursion (adds hiddenPadding at line 106).

Need to verify against C tree-sitter's tree_cursor.c to confirm the correct behavior and add a test with non-zero first-child padding.
